title: 10 best herbal tinctures for bleeding gums

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tinctures helps with bleeding gums because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. The antioxidants present in sage also help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to gum inflammation. Additionally, sage's antibacterial properties help to combat the underlying bacterial infections that can cause bleeding gums. As a result, using herbal sage tinctures can provide natural relief from bleeding gums and promote healthy gums overall.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gum tissue, which can contribute to bleeding gums.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's flavonoids and terpenes have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with bleeding gums, promoting healing and reducing discomfort.
Astringent properties: Sage's tannins and other compounds have astringent properties that can help constrict blood vessels and stop bleeding by reducing the flow of blood to the affected area.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic diterpenic acid that helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums, thereby alleviating bleeding gums.
Carnosic acid: A phenolic diterpenoid that has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce gum inflammation and prevent bleeding.
Ursolic acid: A triterpene with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to reduce gum inflammation, prevent infection, and promote healing, thereby reducing bleeding gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of antioxidants and antibacterial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and fight off infections in the gums.
Stems: They contain salvinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help to reduce bleeding and promote healing in the gums.
Roots: They are used due to their rich content of sesquiterpenes, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to prevent infections and reduce inflammation in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the harvested salvia officinalis leaves and flowers using a dehydrator or low temperature oven at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine 1 part of dried salvia officinalis with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The herb's anti-inflammatory agents, like beta-carotene and lutein, reduce swelling and pain in the gums, which can help to stop bleeding.
Astringent properties: Pot marigold's astringent compounds, such as tannins, constrict blood vessels and help to stop bleeding by reducing the flow of blood to the affected area.

Terpenoids (specifically, triterpenoids): These compounds have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and protect the gums, reducing inflammation and preventing bleeding.

Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in treating gum inflammation and infections.
Stems: The stems, although not as commonly used as flowers and leaves, may still be used in some tinctures due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers in their peak bloom and dry them thoroughly to prevent mold.
Combine 1 part dried calendula officinalis flowers with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks to allow the tincture to macerate.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar to remove the plant material.
Label the tincture and store it in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks before using.

remedy_recipe: Harvest camellia sinensis leaves in the early morning, ideally within 2 hours after sunrise, to capture optimal potency.
Clean the leaves thoroughly, gently removing any dirt or debris, to prevent contamination and preserve quality.

Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discard the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis flowers and leaves on a sunny day in the morning when dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer on paper bags or screens for 1 week.
Chop 200g of dried plant material into small pieces and combine it with 40% ethanol in a ratio of 1:5.
Store the mixture in a dark glass container and steep it in a cool dry place for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a separate container and discard the solids.

remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Matricaria chamomilla contain anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help soothe and calm bleeding gums.

remedy_recipe: Gather 250g of fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers and clean them thoroughly with water to remove any dirt.
Dry the flowers in a warm place for 24 hours to preserve them for tincture making.

Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth to separate the flowers from the liquid and discard the solids.
Bottle the tincture in dark glass bottles and store it in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and other phenolic compounds that help to reduce gum bleeding by contracting blood vessels and promoting hemostasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gentiana lutea roots at peak potency in late summer or early fall weighing about 20% of the total yield.
Dry the harvested roots using air circulation or low heat within 24 hours to prevent mold and bacterial growth.
Cut the dried gentiana lutea roots into small pieces and combine with 50% proof vodka at a ratio of one part herb to four parts solvent.
Store the tincture mixture in a dark glass bottle for four weeks with occasional agitation every seven days for maximum extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container discarding the solids and label for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with bleeding gums because it contains antimicrobial properties that combat periodontal bacteria, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. The astringent compounds in elderberry also help to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding by forming a protective barrier on the gum tissue. Additionally, elder tincture's antioxidant properties may help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to gum disease. By targeting multiple aspects of gum health, elder tinctures can provide natural relief from bleeding gums.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce swelling and alleviate inflammation in the gums, thereby reducing bleeding.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial agents present in elder tincture, like triterpenes and polyphenols, combat bacterial infections that can cause gum bleeding and promote healing of the affected areas.

Antioxidant properties: Elder tincture's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue and contribute to bleeding, while also promoting healthy cell growth and regeneration in the mouth.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the elder plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing gum inflammation and bleeding.
Barks: The barks of the elder plant contain astringent compounds that help to constrict blood vessels and reduce gum bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality sambucus nigra flowers in full bloom and gently rinse them with filtered water.
Combine one part sambucus nigra flowers with two parts vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks to allow for extraction and maceration.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and label them with the date of preparation and contents.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with bleeding gums because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's compounds, such as limonene and anethole, have been shown to reduce plaque formation and inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause gum disease. Additionally, fennel's ability to improve blood circulation helps to deliver oxygen and nutrients to the gums, promoting healthy tissue and reducing inflammation, ultimately leading to a decrease in bleeding.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Fennel tincture's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gums, which can contribute to bleeding.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in fennel tincture, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce swelling and pain in the gums, promoting healing and reducing bleeding.
Antimicrobial properties: Fennel tincture's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial and fungal infections that may contribute to gum disease, which often leads to bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the gums, making them effective in treating bleeding gums.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain anethole, a compound that has antiseptic properties, which helps to prevent infection and promote healing in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried foeniculum vulgare herb in amounts of 50 grams and clean it thoroughly with organic cotton cloth.
Combine the cleaned foeniculum vulgare herb with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a dark glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for four weeks, shaking the jar daily, before straining the liquid through cheesecloth.
Transfer the resulting tincture to a clean amber glass bottle and label it clearly with ingredients dosages preparation times and expiration date.
Store the foeniculum vulgare tincture in a cool dark place for six months or longer before use as desired.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for bleeding gums are liquid extracts made from plants, which have been traditionally used to soothe and heal gum inflammation. These herbal remedies help by reducing swelling, fighting bacteria, and improving blood clotting, thereby stopping bleeding. Examples of effective herbal tinctures include Calendula, Goldenseal, and Echinacea, which can be applied topically or taken orally as a supplement. By using these natural remedies, individuals with bleeding gums can experience relief from discomfort and embarrassment, allowing them to confidently smile again, improving their overall quality of life and self-confidence.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to bleeding gums that are treated with herbal tinctures are those related to oral health, digestive issues, and inflammation. For example, tinctures of goldenseal and myrrh may be used to treat gum abscesses, while peppermint and chamomile tinctures can help soothe mouth sores and reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat. Ginger and turmeric tinctures have anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate digestive issues like bloating and cramps.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with bleeding gums is a blend of Calendula, Echinacea, and Myrrh. Calendula tincture helps to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, while Echinacea supports immune function and fights off underlying infections. Myrrh, with its antimicrobial properties, aids in killing bacteria that can cause gum inflammation. Combining these tinctures can help to soothe and heal bleeding gums, promoting a healthy oral environment.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Iranian journal of basic medical sciences, herbal tinctures are beneficial for bleeding gums due to the effectiveness of certain herbal products in reducing gingival inflammation and bleeding. The study, which reviewed 18 clinical trials on herbal treatments for gingivitis, found that herbal medicines such as pomegranate, aloe, green tea, and miswak, as well as some polyherbal formulations like triphala, significantly reduced plaque index, bleeding index, microbial count, and biomarkers of inflammation. Specifically, the study found that these herbal products could decrease gingival inflammation and bleeding by 10-20% more than conventional treatments, highlighting their potential as a natural remedy for bleeding gums.
